verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Eusebius, surnamed Pamphilus, writing the History of the Church in ten books, closed it with that period of the emperor Constantine, when the persecution which Diocletian had begun against the Christians came to an end.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Also in writing the life of Constantine, this same author has but slightly treated of matters regarding Arius, being more intent on the rhetorical finish of his composition and the praises of the emperor, than on an accurate statement of facts.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now, as we propose to write the details of what has taken place in the churches since his time to our own day, we begin with the narration of the particulars which he has left out, and we shall not be solicitous to display a parade of words, but to lay before the reader what we have been able to collect from documents, and what we have heard from those who were familiar with the facts as they told them.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And since it has an important bearing on the matter in hand, it will be proper to enter into a brief account of Constantine’s conversion to Christianity, making a beginning with this event.
